title: Living in the Van One Year Anniversary Vlog 9-18-2011

transcript.text: So this is my Living in the Van vlog one-year anniversary.
It's the 18th of September now.
I flew back home from Amsterdam on the 16th one year ago,
And pretty much one day after that is when I drove out to Moab
And started living in my van.
So it's been a year I've been living in my van.
And I'm getting better at it.
And what did I learn after a year's worth of living in my van?
Well, for the first month or two, I wasn't working on my timeline.
I think I was there for like three weeks in Moab in the middle of nowhere living in my van.
Well, actually living in Moab too.
And then I moved down to Tucson when it got too cold.
And I was in Tucson for a couple weeks or like a week or something just riding until the bike broke.
And that was in the shop three months.
And it took me about three days after the bike broke to have a gander's at my timeline
And then get sucked into it.
And then I started working on that.
For a few months, maybe even the whole, not the whole time,
But for the first, I guess I was there four months.
For the first like three months, I was nocturnal.
And then I became dayturnal or diurnal.
And I went over to the library and I was spending all day at the library.
And one big lesson living in your van is it's much, much better to be diurnal than nocturnal.
Because if you're nocturnal, you can kind of feel like you're living in your van.
And you want to feel like you're just a normal person.
And if you sleep during the night and you're up during the day,
You can experience life just like a normal person, the sunshine people.
And you can use facilities that are only open during the day for like the library and the gym
And, you know, the coffee shop and stuff.
When I was nocturnal, I'd be kind of like in my car the whole time
Because I was using my little computer.
It was before I had my big computer.
So the battery could last a long time.
I had these two big batteries.
So I could be working on that all night.
And I was drinking and hanging out all night.
And then be in the Denny's all night.
And it's just, it wasn't good.
So being dayturnal or diurnal is way better.
You can be, you can actually love living in the van.
That was back when I was a little bit kind of stressed about,
Oh, am I going to find a place to park?
Where am I going to park?
And now I don't even care.
Now I know.
It's kind of like the longer you go on something without an incident,
The more like relaxed you get.
But yeah, I'm like parked on some like road.
And I know I'm not going to get bugged, you know.
And I'm just, maybe I'll even park here next time
Or just go to a place nearby or whatever.
But it's never too far away to find a cool place to camp.
But yeah, and then I went over to, I was in Phoenix for like three weeks
And I was just working all the time during the day.
But I was working all the time.
And then I went into Las Vegas for like a month, a little over a month,
A month and a week.
And I was working all the time there.
And then I cracked when I was in Las Vegas.
Physically, I started to deteriorate.
Having mainly manifesting itself, not being able to breathe during the day
And mainly at night, but also having the leg pains,
The pains of muscle atrophy pains.
And so I went into Sacramento and I was like,
Oh yeah, fuck this.
I'm going to get a gym membership.
Because, you know, I worked out in the park one day
And I was like, this isn't going to work.
I'm just going to get a gym membership.
Because if I work out, then that means I sweat
And that means I have to have a shower.
And I started showering every day and that was really good.
That was nice.
Before that, I'd be going like three weeks without showering.
And I was working out and I got into shape.
I just kind of got myself into shape.
And then I left Sacramento.
And that's kind of like when I lost it.
I lost and went out of shape again.
And I hadn't been in shape since.
I was up there in Bellingham.
Washington for like a little over a month.
Working, just working, working.
And then for the last week, I've been in northern Seattle,
Just working, working.
Well, kind of walking on the treadmill every day
For like the last week or something.
But now I'm going to start, I'm going to flip.
And my day is going to revolve around exercise
And then fill in the blanks with working on my history timeline
Just organically, you know.
Just go in there and just go, do, do, do, whatever.
Not pay attention to what I did that day.
I'll feel like I did a lot
Because I'd see all the cool stuff that I do.
It's going to be fun.
And I'm going to get into shape.
And that's going to be fun.
I'm going to start doing the rowing every day
Against the girls
And try and catch up to the college girls
Who can vaporize me.
They're like 30% stronger than me.
Even the 15-year-old chicks are like that,
Way stronger than me.
So that's going to be fun.
And also the lifting
And then the walking on the treadmill
And working in between.
I'm really excited about getting into shape.
Yeah.
So predictions for the next year.
Predictions for the next year living in the van.
I'm going to stay here for a few months
Because I quite simply don't have the money
For gas to go anywhere south
Because I have to go way east before I go south
Because I've already done the south or the west.
And I don't have that kind of money.
And we'll see.
I mean, I have the gym membership
So it could be really cold out
And I'll just go in the gym.
And I have a sleeping bag.
I actually have two sleeping bags.
So I think I'll be okay.
And, you know, I have to run my car anyways.
15 minutes a day.
And that's about how much time I need
To like warm up my car in the morning
Or at night or whatever.
So it works.
It'll work.
I think I'll be okay.
And there's tons of gyms.
I can be up here as long as I want.
There's so many freaking cold gyms.
There's like 20 or something.
It's crazy.
I don't know if there's that many
But there's a lot.
And so I am a lot more at ease
And a lot happier about living in the van
Than I was before.
Before it was just like a money-saving thing
And it was a, you know,
I was just focused on the timeline.
But now that I'm diurnal,
Which is really important,
You want to be a day person, dude,
Or if you're living in your van.
Oh, and hopefully in the future
I'm going to start cooking
And that's going to make me spend a lot less.
And I'm going to have to start cooking too
Because after about a month
My metabolism is going to kick into gear again
And I'm going to have to start eating a lot more,
Which would be a lot more expensive
If I eat the way that I eat.
So I'm basically going to eat pasta.
Pasta and oatmeal and bananas
Is what's going to keep me eating a lot cheaply.
Get those carbs in me
And that'll be good enough.
And I'll do the protein powder,
But not that much.
You know, I don't really want to get big anyways
Because if you're big and heavy
You can't do the body weight exercises and stuff.
So, yeah.
Yeah, I'm more interested in just losing fat
Really than gaining muscle.
And, yeah, for the next year I'll be here
And then I'll move up north.
I'm excited.
But I'm excited to get into shape.
In one year I predict my vlog will be
Basically just fucking rocking shape.
I'm at another place.
Working on my timeline every day,
Day after day.
Pretty much going to be a routine,
But I like routine.
All right, peace out.
